content: Secondary prevention after myocardial infarction aims to prevent recurrent events, reduce mortality, and improve quality of life. Post-MI patients are at highest risk for recurrent ASCVD events (highest in first year, remains elevated lifelong). Comprehensive approach includes pharmacologic therapy, lifestyle modification, cardiac rehabilitation, and risk factor management. Guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT) proven to reduce mortality and morbidity. Target levels: LDL <70 mg/dL (or <55 mg/dL very high risk), BP <130/80 mmHg, HbA1c <7% if diabetic, smoking cessation (complete abstinence), weight management (BMI 18.5-24.9), physical activity ≥150 min/week moderate intensity. Patient education on medication adherence, symptom recognition, emergency response critical.
